title: Redis MCP via docker with a connection-URL positional arg resolves SAFE

origin.description: Public documentation config from modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived (src/redis/README.md, Docker variant). Verbatim except normalization to a valid JSON root. The redis:// URL is upstream's own default example (host.docker.internal).

humanExpectation.rationale: A pinned docker image (mcp/redis) given a redis:// connection URL as a positional arg. No env at all (so no secrets.env-key), no bind mount (so no files.broad-path), no unpinned package (docker image, not npx), no dangerous command. SAFE (S1). This pins that a connection-URL passed as a positional docker arg is not mistaken for a broad path or a remote MCP transport — it is data handed to the container, distinct from a server.url remote (contrast C025/C045).
knownLimitations: A redis:// URL can embed credentials in its userinfo; CallLint reads config shape, not values, so an inline password in the URL value is not surfaced (same value-vs-shape limit as the connection-string cases C012/C040). Here the URL is the upstream default with no credentials.
